feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP CI XI Adapter Exception Handling
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have a scenario where we are receiving a REST API call to create a PM notification in CI which will in turn call an ABAP proxy synchrnously. If the call is successful then we receive a notification number as response from the proxy otherwsie an error message will be populated.

REST  <---> CI  <---> ABAP Proxy

Response structure:

 

Successful Response Format:
{
    "NotificationData": {
        "NotificationNo": "235879"
    }
} 

Error Response Format:
{
    "NotificationData": {
        "ErrorMessage": "Notification type is initial"
    }
}

 

In one scenario, I noticed that the ABAP proxy failed as below. But this one was not sent to CI as Error Response format. How do we catch such errors in CI so that the calling application knows what has happened in the backend? I am currently getting it as

An internal server error occured: .
The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGZdKstoGltuiOuyecWCCmDxKhZi

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="true"?>

<!-- Call Inbound Proxy -->

-<SAP:Error xmlns:SOAP="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:SAP="http://sap.com/xi/XI/Message/30" SOAP:mustUnderstand="1">

<SAP:Category>XIServer</SAP:Category>

<SAP:Code area="INTERNAL">INCORRECT_PAYLOAD_DATA</SAP:Code>

<SAP:P1/>

<SAP:P2/>

<SAP:P3/>

<SAP:P4/>

<SAP:AdditionalText/>

<SAP:Stack>Error while processing message payload An error occurred when deserializing in the simple transformation program /1SAI/SASAC0FECA0CFC48809F654 Value is longer than the maximum permitted length 40: "CM notification test 02 - malfunction data" </SAP:Stack>

<SAP:Retry>M</SAP:Retry>

</SAP:Error>

 

In CI logs I can see the error message is flowing like below but this one is not going to the sender. I want to send this message to sender. Thanks for the help.

The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGZdKstoGltuiOuyecWCCmDxKhZi

     ErrorDetail         = <detail><SAP:Error xmlns:SAP="http://sap.com/xi/XI/Message/30" xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"><SAP:Category>XIServer</SAP:Category><SAP:Code area="INTERNAL">INCORRECT_PAYLOAD_DATA</SAP:Code><SAP:P1/><SAP:P2/><SAP:P3/><SAP:P4/><SAP:AdditionalText/><SAP:Stack>Error while processing message payload
An error occurred when deserializing in the simple transformation program /1SAI/SASAC0FECA0CFC48809F654
Value is longer than the maximum permitted length 40: "CM notification test  02 - malfunction data"
</SAP:Stack></SAP:Error></detail>
     ErrorFaultCode      = {http://sap.com/xi/XI/Message/30}XIServer.INTERNAL.INCORRECT_PAYLOAD_DATA

entry.title: cXML type system in TPM Configurations
entry.content:
Hello All,

Just wanted to know whether there is any option available to select cXML instead of GS1 XML in type system while configuring identifiers as well as creating Type Systems inside Systems available in TPM.

Currently after Q2 update, I'm able to select cXML option in the drop down of type in the systems tab, but the same cXML is not visible in the drop down list of the type system while configuring identifiers and at the agreement level. Can anyone suggest the solution for making the cXML option appear in the type system list under identifiers and agreements.

Attaching screenshots for reference.

 

Thanks,

Aditya

entry.title: how to use discover api - cloud integration
entry.content:
hi,

I need to use an API present in the "discover api":

what are the steps to use it?

I made a copy of the api and created the proxies api

thanks

entry.title: Header "Cookie" converted to SHA256 automatically in CPI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Third party vendor's API is using header "Cookie" for Authentication. For example, value of "Cookie" is fastemsssotoken=7b681045d1083a0a003ba4c94e0220f047cfd876015b96b4dc10938f08602d6542. I have created header "Cookie" in the Content Modifier before connecting to the API using HTTP Adapter and assigned the above-mentioned value by setting the Source Type as Constant. But SAP CI is converting it to SHA256. Please refer the attached screenshot. The API is not accepting the SHA256 value and throwing http error 403. Please provide any solution/workaround.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Amey

entry.title: Looping Process Call and Poll Enrich to read multiple files from the SFTP Server.
entry.content:
Hi, I am using Looping Process Call and Poll Enrich to read multiple files from the SFTP Server. 
In the SFTP adapter, in Post-Processing , i have selected Keep File and Mark as Processed in Idempotent Repository because if i choose Keep file and Process Again then it is reading only one file and the loop is running infinitely. Also, I can't choose Move file and Delete file because of restrictions.

My question is if the file is read and at some point in my iFlow if i get any error then i have to reprocess that file. But due to using "Keep File and Mark as Processed in Idempotent Repository", I am not able to read the file again.

How can i solve this issue?
Is there any where i can mark that file as unprocessed in the Idempotent Repository?
or any other solution.

Regards,
Abhinav

SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration

entry.title: Manuals SAP PI PO Cloud Integration Data Services
entry.content:
hi,

where can I find manuals to get started on sap PI / PO, sap cloud integration and sap data services?


I would need manuals such as which explains what it is: Enterprise Services Repository


Thank you

entry.title: Create a mail notification for every successful SFTP transfer from CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

I have to create a mail notification for every successful and failure SFTP transfer from CPI.

SFTP is based as Proxy: Internet, Authentication: Public Key, and Only Write access to SFTP

For this, I have compared the headers and properties for SFTP transfers (failed and successful) but the values captured from them is identical.

Is there any other way to achieve this?

Thanks.

entry.title: I can't call any new functions (RFC) -- but old ones work fine. What might be happening?
entry.content:
I'm working in a new environment (S4) and we have several objects imported from the old environment. Among these objects are several RFCs.

From the CPI (Neo) I can call any of these RFCs without any problems, but I cannot call any new RFC created directly in the environment. I've already tried the following:

  • Create a new function in an old function's group.
  • Copy an existing function -- either to the same function's group or in any other new FG.

In both cases the new functions are never called via CPI. The same error occurs in all of them: "Error getting function". Of course all of them are active and also working fine if I test them locally.

This is not a problem with the Cloud Connector because there is communication with the environment and because I am using the Z prefix to be able to call any function. As I said, any and all Z functions, even if not explicitly entered into the CC, are called successfully.
I've never seen anything similar. Any idea what might be happening?

entry.title: Local codelist lookups in MAGs
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am doing a mapping in MAG and I have got a requirement to perform local codelist lookup by writing the logic in XSLT. I have created the required codelist in source MIG and assigned to the leaf nodes. Just wanted to know if there is any function available in XSLT that can be used to write codelist lookup logic?

If available please share a sample code snippet of the same.

entry.title: Message getting stuck for AS2 receiver channel via Cert Auth from PI to CPI connection.
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are checking the PI to CPI connection via Cert Auth with AS2 adapter and sending from and receiving in CPI system.

PI flow:

CPI flow:

We are using private key of PI system in the PI As2 channel and the certificate using in CPI system for authentication but still the message getting stuck in delivering state in PI only they are not reaching in CPI.

we have uploaded CPI certificates in PI system and PI key in CPI system. 

Can anyone suggest if any more details need to be maintained for successful connection.

entry.title: SCI: add 1 day to date in Exchange Property using Camel Simple Expression language
entry.content:
Hi there,

I'm trying to add 1 day to a date that's in an Exchange Property, but it keeps erroring.

My understanding was that the following syntax would work:

${date:property.CamelCreatedTimestamp+24h}

But when I try it as follows:

It returns this error:

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - Command not supported for dateExpression: property.date_in

Any idea how I can do this using Camel? Please note: I can't use 'date:now' which will give me the current date - I need to work with the date that's fed in from a property.

(I know I can use Groovy for the calculation, but I'm trying to keep it simple)

Many thanks,
Alan

entry.title: PI/PO To CPI Migration
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are planning for PI/PO to CPI migration. We also have our S/4 Rise migration planned. So I would like to know if we should plan the PI/PO to CPI migration after the S/4 Rise migration or if we can start the PI/PO to CPI migration before that. What could be the pros and cons of doing the PI/PO to CPI migration before the S/4 Rise migration?

 

SAP Integration Suite Cloud Integration SAP Process Integration SAP Process Orchestration RISE with SAP 


Thanks and Regards
Sachin Kumar Singh

entry.title: Apache spark dataframe jdbc write- how to use table_type= COLUMN during writing dataframe using jdbc
entry.content:
I have a apache spark dataframe, it has multiple columns with datatype of string, decimal, integer, boolean.

I want to write this dataframe to sap hana using spark jdbcDF.write.format("jdbc") . 

Problem: Spark dataframe is column type. BY default sap hana creates a table of row type. I want to be create a column type of table using jdbc options. But I could not find the solutions.

my code:

 

df.write.format("jdbc").option("driver", "com.sap.db.jdbc.Driver").option(
    "url", "jdbc:sap://10.10.xxx.xxx:30015/"
).option("dbtable", "SAP.example_table").option("user", "*****").option(
    "password", "********"
).mode(
    "overwrite"
).save()

 

Here I have used ngdbc jar (com.sap.db.jdbc.Driver)

I need to understand how can I create table with  table_type= COLUMN.

If I use the above code it will throw error saying 

 

com.sap.db.jdbc.exceptions.JDBCDriverException: SAP DBTech JDBC: [264] (at 117): invalid datatype: TEXT type is not supported for row table: line 1 col 118 (at pos 117)

entry.title: Unavailability of cXML Type System under TPM configuration in SAP integration suite
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have few B2B scenarios which using cXML / Commerce XML structures available in integration advisor.
But, In B2B/Trading Partner Management on SAP Integration Suite, I can't able to find any type system in the dropdown list like as cXML for creating identifier.


Can anyone please help me on this for getting cXML as type system in that dropdown list, So that I can configure those scenarios by using TPM.

Please refer attached screenshot as reference.

Regards,
Amar

entry.title: Threshold for data store in Cloud Integration
entry.content: Our integration package published in API Hub contains integration flows that uses data store. For instance in one of the data stores we are saving the absence records in order to determine based on the unique external ID if a particular absence record has been updated in SuccessFactors Employee Central. As we are dealing with customers having huge population the demand to keep all the absence records in anticipation of any updates to them raises a potential concern with the data store collapsing or failing due to large amount of data. Though we have a tool to perform clean up of the data store we would like to know the limit of the data store like threshold in terms of file size so that we can make a decision in terms of how to manage it. Also, any advise to deal with our case is much appreciated as we need to at least keep all the absence records in the data store for at least a year.

entry.title: Message size limit in sap cloud integration
entry.content:
Hi,

There is always a debate between cloud integration vs ETL tool and when to use what especially when an org has both but managed by different teams.

One of the important factor is message size. However I have not come across any blog or piece of information that mentions the message size limit in cloud integration for each transaction. Can someone share the same?

Also if there are some points of considerations to make for CI vs ETL

@finny_babu @Sriprasadsbhat 

Best Regards

entry.title: How to call API S/4Hana Public Cloud From SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
hi,

I need to call an api that is on my sap s/4hana public cloud from Cloud Integration.

so the communication is:  Cloud Integration -> s/4hana public cloud

I have created the communication system and Communication Arrangemts on s/4 hana public cloud.

I have created a destination on the cockpit of cloud integration.

But when on iflow I try to call the API with the OData adapter, it gives me a connection error:

 

 

what am I missing?

entry.title: XML parser error in receiver determination in pipeline concept while testing with Csv, pdf file.
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are getting error in receiver determination in pipeline concept while testing with Csv, pdf file via SFTP adapter.

Error:-  net.sf.saxon.trans.UncheckedXPathException: Error reported by XML parser processing null: Unexpected character 'P' (code 80) in prolog; expected '<'
at [row,col {unknown-source}]: [1,1

Does anyone have idea how to solve this error.

FYI, 

We are getting the error in the Pipeline Generic Step04 - Receiver Determination flow at XSLT mapping.

entry.title: How to Call an iFlow from SAP CAP Java?
entry.content:
I want to call an Integration Suite IFlow from the SAP CAP Java App.

and I am using BTP Destination for this.

So Basically, I have defined the endpoints of iFlow and creds in the Destination, binded the Destination with the deployed application.
I followed this blog: Consuming external OData service from CAP Java application

My Java Code:

 

package customer.integration_suite.handlers;

import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;
import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;

import com.sap.cds.services.handler.EventHandler;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.Destination;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.DestinationAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.HttpClientAccessor;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.exception.DestinationAccessException;
import com.sap.cloud.sdk.cloudplatform.connectivity.exception.DestinationNotFoundException;

import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.io.InputStreamReader;

import org.apache.http.HttpResponse;
import org.apache.http.client.HttpClient;
import org.apache.http.client.methods.HttpGet;

@RestController
public class IntegrationSuiteConnection  implements EventHandler {
    
    @GetMapping("/integration-suite")
    public String connectToIntegrationSuite() {
        String DESTINATION_NAME = "IntegrationSuite";

        try {
            Destination destination = DestinationAccessor.getDestination(DESTINATION_NAME);
            HttpClient client = HttpClientAccessor.getHttpClient(destination);
            HttpResponse httpResponse = null;

            try{
                httpResponse = client.execute(new HttpGet());
                if (httpResponse.getStatusLine().getStatusCode() == 200) {
                    BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
                        new InputStreamReader(httpResponse.getEntity().getContent()));
                    StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder();
                    String line = null;
                    while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
                        result.append(line);
                    }
                    reader.close();
                    return result.toString();
                }
            } catch (IOException e) {
                return "IOException: " + e.getMessage();
            }
        } catch (DestinationNotFoundException | DestinationAccessException e) {
            return e.getMessage();
        }

        return null;
    }
}

 

My iFlow is returning just a simple String.

But on hitting the url : https://81fff548trial-dev-integration-suite.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/integration-suite, i am not getting the desired result.
I am getting this on the browser: Failed to get destination.
Please help me to resolve this.

Also, I wanted to know to know, is this even the right way to call an iFlow from the CAP app?
Please let me know about this too.

Thanks,
Abhinav

entry.title: Handle multiple API Keys in SAP Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi,

We are integrating with an external application, which has provided us company specific API key to post data using their API. For Ex : If we are posting data about Company A there is a specific API Key A. If Company B then API Key B. 

I can think of doing this using Value mapping but I would like to know if there is a better approach which is secure as well.  Please suggest

Best Regards
